issues.detail: The phrase "accepts in-thread AND creates the linked task" is not fully negative-tested. The suite omits volunteer acceptance outside the thread and an in-thread acceptance paired with an unlinked or wrong-project task.
issues.fix: Add P0 fixtures proving both cases remain out of scope until the acceptance is in the project thread and the volunteer-created task is correctly linked.

issues.detail: AT-025.03 requires explicit NGO acknowledgment when the addition is recorded and rejects the addition otherwise, but the requirement permits that acknowledgment any time "before completion." This also clashes with AT-025.02's immediate scope entry and AT-025.11's pending-acknowledgment scenario.
issues.fix: At recording, require a choice between top-priority and proposed-optional. Make optional/non-blocking effective only when NGO acknowledgment is recorded before completion; do not require acknowledgment at initial recording.

issues.detail: "The task predates any attributed burn" is not a valid oracle for "before material fuel is spent." REQ-034 permits unattributed and taskless usage, treats attribution as soft telemetry, and records tokens rather than fuel, so material pre-acceptance spend could occur while this test passes.
issues.fix: Use a known material-work fixture and authoritative metered-request/provider-usage chronology to prove both acceptance and linked-task creation precede that work, regardless of its attribution binding.

issues.detail: Calling the outcome observable does not define how an automated judge determines that a discussion is "active," that an ask "waits," or that it was directed elsewhere. An informal comment stream can satisfy the prose without exposing any asserted state.
issues.fix: Define auditable thread/project artifacts and ordering for each allowed outcome, such as a recorded queued disposition or a distinct follow-up-project identifier, with timestamps proving only one ask is handled at a time.

issues.detail: The qualifier "per project" is uncovered. A faulty global one-discussion limit would pass the existing single-project fixture.
issues.fix: Add a two-project fixture proving each project may simultaneously have its own active scope-addition discussion.

issues.detail: The mandatory assertion that a normal thread notification fires belongs to REQ-015/016, not isolated REQ-025; the PRD is conflict-check-only and is not a coverage source. This round-1 edit makes REQ-025 completion depend on another suite's behavior.
issues.fix: Remove notification delivery as a REQ-025 pass condition. Assert only that arrival of the idea, including any ordinary notification governed elsewhere, causes no auto-assignment, task change, queue jump, or blocking interruption.

issues.detail: "NGO-facing scope-addition surface" is undefined, while the structured CR surface is explicitly deferred. The test neither identifies an existing observable channel nor proves the NGO was actually shown the disclosure.
issues.fix: Anchor the assertion to the existing comment-thread addition flow or an auditable concierge disclosure, and verify the required text is visible/delivered without requiring a dedicated CR surface.
